title: The deadly cheetah trafficking between Somaliland and the Gulf
subhead: The feline is highly sought after by wealthy collectors of wild animals, who are numerous on the Arabian Peninsula. Across the way, in the Horn of Africa, Somaliland has become the main hub of a thriving trafficking network, even as the species itself is under threat.

title: Israeli army says 'struck senior Hezbollah commander' in Beirut
Trump says US to leave Iran 'very soon,' deal or not: 'We're finishing the job'
'In their conflict with Iran, the US and Israel are pursuing divergent war aims'
subhead: While Benjamin Netanyahu seeks to topple a regime that the vast majority of Israelis view as an existential threat, Donald Trump is driven by absolute mercantilism, geopolitical expert Frédéric Encel argues for Le Monde.

title: French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship now available online
subhead: Starting on March 30, more than 180,000 records became accessible on the website of the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons (OFPRA), thanks to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of 40 years of exile in France.

title: Robert Capa, a photographer in the heat of the action, at the Musée de la Libération de Paris
subhead: A compelling exhibition, which features a montage of archival footage capturing Capa at work, traces the extraordinary life of the Hungarian cofounder of Magnum Photos.

title: The French cadets suspected of setting a boat ablaze in Japan
subhead: An investigation is underway into how four cadets from France's elite Saint-Cyr military academy came to be accused of setting a boat on fire while on leave in Okinawa in 2024. The incident has fueled local distrust of military personnel on the island.

title: 'France and Japan need a reciprocal access agreement to strengthen their military cooperation'

Ireland gives Trump a lesson on Europe
subhead: During the traditional Saint Patrick's Day visit to the White House by the Irish prime minister, Micheál Martin defended his British counterpart and praised the virtues of Europe in the face of criticism from the US president.

title: Spice up your life! Five Paris restaurants where you can eat spicy food
subhead: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

title: Revealed: Satellite image shows mysterious cargo at Iranian nuclear site in 2025
subhead: Le Monde reveals an image of a convoy at the gates of a major underground nuclear complex in Iran. According to several experts, it could be part of the country's stockpile of enriched uranium.

title: France has yet to find a forward-looking political discourse on racism
'What if Trump and Netanyahu saved the Islamic Republic of Iran by claiming to overthrow it?'
'As he approached the sands of the Arabian-Persian Gulf, Trump fell prey to a common phenomenon in these lands: mirages'
